stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 25 subscribers. The sample mean is 482.4 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 121.6 Mbps. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 482.4 Mbps. 95% CI = [432.2, 532.6]. Point estimate: 482.4 Mbps.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
